evidence: Reviewed 2026-06-25 one more bounded official Idaho education pass across the four remaining district-owned hosts plus their current machine-readable inventory surfaces. Jefferson remains positively recovered from district-owned `special-education`, `special-services`, `section-504`, and `student-services` leaves. Oneida remains positively recovered from the district-owned Child Find PDF. But the residual four districts now finalize as wrong-role survivors rather than unknown roots. Camas still only exposes a district-owned `Contact Information` leaf with district address and phone, while both `robots.txt` and `sitemap.xml` 404. Clark now exposes a real public XML sitemap at `https://www.clarkcountyschools161.org/fs/pages/sitemap` plus exact district-owned `Contact Us` and `Title IX` leaves, but the sitemap inventory still yields no `special-education`, `special-services`, `student-services`, `section-504`, `child-find`, or procedural-safeguards page. Fremont now exposes a live sitemap at `https://www.sd215.net/sitemap.xml`, but that sitemap only yields generic routes like `o/fo`, `staff`, `documents`, `ourdistrict`, `title-ix`, and district forms; direct bounded probes for `/page/special-education`, `/page/special-services`, `/page/student-services`, `/page/section-504`, and `/page/child-find` all 404. Shoshone now exposes a live WordPress sitemap index at `https://shoshonesd.org/wp-sitemap.xml`, but the page, post, and user sitemap feeds still only materialize generic district, board, agenda, and newsletter pages; direct bounded probes for `/special-education/`, `/student-services/`, `/special-services/`, `/section-504/`, and `/child-find/` all 404. Idaho therefore remains blocked because the remaining district-owned roots and their live sitemap inventories still materialize only wrong-role or generic leaves rather than local special-education routing.

Reviewed 2026-06-23 bounded live Idaho DHW confirmation on the official root plus the existing office-leaf packet. The public root at https://healthandwelfare.idaho.gov/offices is live with title `Find a Service Location` and still preserves exact city office cards like Caldwell Office in HTML, but it exposes zero county terms or county-served fields. The exact office leaf https://healthandwelfare.idaho.gov/dhw/caldwell-office is live with title `Caldwell Office`, confirming that the packet is grounded in real reviewable DHW office leaves. Idaho county-local routing therefore splits cleanly into 17 safe county-to-exact-office replacements plus one Canyon split that still rejects Nampa as SWITC-only, while 27 counties remain blocked because the public DHW stack still exposes no truthful county-to-office contract for them.
